name: Ardabil Carpet
primary_sources: Los Angeles County Museum of Art
Historical records from the Safavid period

visual_attributes.texture: Dense wool pile with a silk foundation
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: 34 ft 3 in × 17 ft 6+7⁄8 in for the London carpet; 23 ft 7 in × 13 ft 1+1⁄2 in for the Los Angeles carpet
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Central medallion surrounded by ornate designs and a border of cartouches

domain_specific_visual.DESIGN_INFLUENCE: Resembles the interior dome of the Sheikh Lotfollah Mosque

functional_attributes.primary_function: Decorative and symbolic representation of paradise

relationships.description: The larger Ardabil Carpet is housed in this museum.

relationships.target: Los Angeles County Museum of Art
relationships.description: The smaller Ardabil Carpet is housed in this museum.
The carpets were created during this ruler's reign.

associated_entities: Sheikh Safi al-Din Khānegāh and Shrine Ensemble

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Originally from Iran, now in the UK and USA
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents the pinnacle of Persian carpet weaving and Islamic art
primary_sources: Los Angeles County Museum of Art records

contrastive_constraints: NOT unrelated to Islamic art and Persian culture
